finding_results.evidence: chapters/chapter_07.md:275; chapters/chapter_07.md:277; chapters/chapter_07.md:279; chapters/chapter_07.md:287; chapters/chapter_07.md:291; chapters/chapter_07.md:301; chapters/chapter_07.md:307
finding_results.notes: Five changes made. (1) Replaced the two summarized Ember responses (lines 275-279) with concrete on-screen message excerpts showing Ember's near-term/complex distinction and its safety-limit language, so the negotiation's reciprocation and boundaries are now legible from the page rather than narrated. (2) Removed the narrator meta-comment 'Even now each sentence arrives complete, every clause fitted into place' that reinforced the written-about feeling. (3) Replaced the thesis-line 'Meaning is secondary. What matters is whether it yields leverage' and the clean Viktor/resistance tally with live conditional thinking that shows Chen Wei self-correcting mid-thought ('No. Let me restate that.') and referencing the specific parameters Ember disclosed, ending with qualification ('I did not have this morning') rather than an aphorism. (4) Added a physical grounding beat (thumb on taped glasses) to Chen Wei's follow-up response and shortened it slightly. (5) Changed 'I am always careful' to 'I am being careful' — present-tense assertion rather than characterological curtain-line. Chen Wei's formal low-contraction register and framework vocabulary are preserved throughout.

summary: Single finding addressed. The Chen Wei negotiation scene now puts three Ember messages on screen (up from one), showing the exchange's offer, reciprocation, and limit directly. Chen Wei's spoken dialogue replaces thesis-speech with live conditional reasoning, visible self-correction, and qualified conclusions while preserving his formal register and framework vocabulary. The narrator no longer comments on his sentence completeness. Surrounding chapter text untouched; heading contract, continuity, payoffs (Lena cafeteria takeover, loading dock as chokepoint), and all other scene architecture preserved.
